input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
The most likely cause is that your page's **`BindingContext` is never set**, so the `{Binding}` expressions have nothing to resolve against.

**Fix — set the BindingContext**, either in XAML or code-behind:

**Option A: Code-behind (common)**
```csharp
public partial class MyPage : ContentPage
{
    public MyPage(MyViewModel vm)
    {
        InitializeComponent();
        BindingContext = vm;
    }
}
```

**Option B: XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

**Also verify** that your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit), otherwise the UI won't update when property values change after initial load.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values. Looking at the XAML, the most likely issue is that the `BindingContext` is never set.

But there's another common issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, so the UI doesn't update when values change. However, the user says the properties "have values," which suggests they're set — the labels just don't show them.

The primary issue from the XAML alone: there's no `BindingContext` being set. The bindings reference `Title` and `Description`, but without a `BindingContext`, they resolve to nothing.


The most straightforward fix is to set the `BindingContext` to the ViewModel instance, either in the code-behind or in XAML, so the bindings can actually resolve to the properties.
